title: Bisaldeo temple
tagline: Bisaldeo temple — spiritual town of Rajasthan

overview.short: Bisaldeo temple, also known as Bisaldev temple or Bisal Deoji's temple, is a Hindu temple in Bisalpur, India.
overview.description: Bisaldeo temple, also known as Bisaldev temple or Bisal Deoji's temple, is a Hindu temple in Bisalpur, India. It is located beside the Bisalpur Dam on the Banas River, in the Tonk district of Rajasthan state. The temple is dedicated to Gokarneshvara, an aspect of Shiva. A Monument of National Importance, it was commissioned by the 12th century Chahamana ruler Vigraharaja IV, who is also known as Bisal Deo.

topPlaces.name: Bisalpur Dam
topPlaces.description: Bisalpur Dam is a gravity dam on the Banas River near Bisalpur in Tonk district, Rajasthan, India. The dam was completed in 1999 for the purpose of irrigation and water supply.

topPlaces.name: Dai River (India)
topPlaces.description: River Dai originates in the southeastern slopes of the Aravalli Range near Nasirabad Tehsil of Ajmer District. It flows southeast for about 40 km and east for about 56 km in Ajmer District, and for a short reach through Tonk District, before joining the Banas River near Bisalpur village in Tonk District.

topPlaces.name: Beesalpur
topPlaces.description: Beesalpur is a village in Deoli tehsil, Tonk district, Rajasthan, India, located at 31 km from main Deoli city. The population was 4,106 at the 2011 Indian census.

items.note: Bisalpur Dam is a gravity dam on the Banas River near Bisalpur in Tonk district, Rajasthan, India. The dam was completed in 1999 for the purpose of irrigation and water supply.

River Dai originates in the southeastern slopes of the Aravalli Range near Nasirabad Tehsil of Ajmer District. It flows southeast for about 40 km and east for about 56 km in Ajmer District, and for a short reach through Tonk District, before joining the Banas River near Bisalpur village in Tonk District.
Beesalpur is a village in Deoli tehsil, Tonk district, Rajasthan, India, located at 31 km from main Deoli city. The population was 4,106 at the 2011 Indian census.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bisaldeo temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bisaldeo temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bisaldeo temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kota Airport (94 km) and the nearest railway station is Kota Junction (90 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bisaldeo temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bisaldeo temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bisaldeo temple?
faq.a: Stays in Bisaldeo temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bisaldeo temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Bisalpur Dam, Dai River (India), Beesalpur.
